summary: Build-up layer: deployable mining drones, hired NPC traders, owned/built outposts, basic fleet command. All passive yields expressed as a fraction of an explicit active-income reference curve A(T); net passive is hard-capped below active play.
mechanics: Active-income reference A(T): designer-fixed curve of credits/min a competent player earns in active play at progression tier T. T=1:~250 cr/min, T=2:~600, T=3:~1400, T=4:~3200, T=5:~7000. Stored as data table state.balance.activeRefByTier. Every passive source's net yield is sized as a fraction of A(T_player).

GLOBAL PASSIVE CAP: net passive income (after upkeep + expected losses) is clamped so passiveNetPerMin <= passiveCapFrac * A(T_player), passiveCapFrac=0.45. Excess gross above the cap is converted to 'overflow' at 25% efficiency (still rewarding scaling but with sharply diminishing returns), so stacking automation never reaches active rates.

THREE ACCRUAL TYPES. Continuous (drones, outposts): rate per second added to a capped buffer each tick (delta = rate*dt). Discrete (traders): profit credited as an event on route-cycle completion (cycleTime seconds); loss roll per cycle. Offline catch-up: on load, elapsedAway seconds (capped at OFFLINE_CAP=4h=14400s) are simulated in one coarse pass per source.

MINING DRONES: player deploys N drones near an asteroid field (within deployRange of player at deploy time). Each drone auto-targets nearest live asteroid in field, flies to it, mines at mineRate (u/s) of that asteroid's ore type into a SHARED drone-group buffer of capacity bufferCap. When buffer full, drones idle (or auto-return if autoReturn set). Player flies into group radius to 'collect' (transfers buffer ore to ship cargo, subject to cargo space) or recalls (despawns drones, banks buffer to nearest owned outpost/station if linked). Drones consume fuel (drone-fuel units/s) and lose durability on combat hits; at fuel=0 or durability=0 the drone is LOST (destroyed) — this is the attention cost.

DRONE FUEL/DURABILITY: each drone has fuelMax and burns fuelRate u/s while active. Player must recall+refuel (pay refuelCost) before fuel runs out. Durability drops only under attack (pirates may target undefended drone groups). Lost drone = lost asset (no refund). Forces periodic check-ins.

HIRED NPC TRADERS: player hires a trader ship (one-time hireCost) and assigns it a ROUTE = ordered pair/loop of two known stations (A->B->A). Each cycle the trader buys low at A, sells high at B using current economy prices, nets profit, and credits the player on cycle completion. Trader takes upkeepPerMin credit drain whether or not it completes. Each cycle rolls a loss chance based on route danger; on loss the trader (and its in-transit cargo investment) is destroyed by pirates.

TRADER ROUTE PROFIT is computed from the live economy: profit = cargoVol * (sellPrice_B - buyPrice_A) * tradeEff - fuelCost. Repeated cycles on the same route gradually depress the spread (the trader's own volume moves prices via the economy subsystem) so a single route self-limits; player must diversify routes — a management cost.

OWNED OUTPOSTS: player BUYS an existing outpost slot or BUILDS one at a discovered build-site (sector-anchored). Outpost has a recipe: consumes input commodity at inRate and produces output commodity at outRate into a capped storage buffer (storageCap). Player (or a hired trader assigned to it) hauls output to market to realize credits, OR outpost can be set to auto-sell to local station at a 20% price penalty. Outposts have upkeepPerMin and can be RAIDED (loss event) if defense < incoming raid strength.

OUTPOST UPGRADES: each outpost has level 1..5; upgrading raises outRate, storageCap, and defense, costs upgradeCost(level), and raises upkeep. Outpost defense reduces raid success probability and can be reinforced by assigning a fleet ship on 'guard'.

BASIC FLEET COMMAND: player owns up to fleetCap ships (fleetCap scales with progression: 2 at T1 up to 8 at T5). Each owned ship gets one ORDER: mine (join a drone-style mining op), trade (run an assigned route like a hired trader but using an owned/better ship), escort (follow player, add to player combat), guard (station at an outpost or drone-group to cut loss probability). Orders are issued from the Fleet UI; one order per ship; switching orders has a redeployTime delay.

ESCORT/GUARD COMPOSITION: a ship on 'guard' at an outpost or drone group multiplies that asset's effective defense, reducing its loss/raid probability (see formulas). This is how fleet command composes with the loss-risk system — defense spending trades against income loss.

ATTENTION/MANAGEMENT COST (anti-idle enforcement): (1) drones must be recalled+refueled before fuel runs out or are lost; (2) trader routes go 'hot' as danger rises and as spreads collapse, forcing manual re-routing; (3) outposts get raided and need defense/restocking; (4) total upkeep drain scales super-linearly with fleet size so unmanaged sprawl bleeds credits. None of these resolve themselves favorably without player action.

UPKEEP DRAIN: summed upkeep of all automation is deducted from state.credits each minute (applied per-tick as upkeep*dt/60). If credits hit 0 and upkeep cannot be paid, assets enter 'distressed' state: drones power down (buffer frozen, fuel still bleeds), traders stop, outposts halt production; after distressGrace=120s of unpaid upkeep, one random distressed asset is repossessed (removed) per grace period — a soft failure, not a hard wipe.

OFFLINE / AWAY ACCRUAL: on game load, compute elapsedAway=now-state.meta.lastSaveTime, clamp to OFFLINE_CAP=14400s. Run one coarse pass: drones add min(mineRate*elapsed, bufferCap) per group; outposts add min(outRate*elapsed, storageCap); traders complete floor(elapsed/cycleTime) cycles each with an aggregated expected-loss roll; upkeep for elapsed is deducted. Offline yield additionally multiplied by OFFLINE_EFF=0.6 so being present is always better.
stateOwned: automation.drones: DroneGroup[] — each {id, sectorId, fieldId, originPos:{x,z}, count, tier, sharedBuffer:{oreType,vol}, bufferCap, fuel, fuelMax, durability, durabilityMax, autoReturn:bool, state:'mining'|'returning'|'idle'|'distressed'}

automation.traders: Trader[] — each {id, tier, route:{fromStationId,toStationId}, cycleProgress:0..1, cycleTime, cargoVol, lastCycleProfit, upkeepPerMin, state:'enroute'|'trading'|'idle'|'distressed', hotness:0..1}

automation.outposts: Outpost[] — each {id, sectorId, pos:{x,z}, recipeId, level, storage:{good,vol}, storageCap, defense, upkeepPerMin, autoSell:bool, raidCooldown, state:'producing'|'halted'|'raided'|'distressed'}
automation.fleet: FleetShip[] — each {id, shipDefId, order:'mine'|'trade'|'escort'|'guard'|'idle', targetRef:{kind,refId}, redeployTimer, hp, state}
automation.fleetCap: int — max owned ships, derived from progression tier
automation.balance: {activeRefByTier:number[], passiveCapFrac:0.45, overflowEff:0.25, offlineEff:0.6, offlineCapSec:14400, distressGraceSec:120}
automation.accumulators: {creditBuffer:number, upkeepDebt:number} — fractional per-tick credit accrual/drain carried between ticks
content: DRONE Mk1 | tier 1 | mineRate 0.8 u/s | bufferCap 60 u | fuelMax 240 s-equiv | fuelRate 1.0/s | durabilityMax 40 | deployRange 350 wu | cost 4000 cr | upkeep 6 cr/min
DRONE Mk2 | tier 2 | mineRate 1.6 u/s | bufferCap 120 u | fuelMax 360 | fuelRate 1.0/s | durabilityMax 70 | deployRange 400 wu | cost 12000 cr | upkeep 14 cr/min
DRONE Mk3 | tier 3 | mineRate 3.0 u/s | bufferCap 240 u | fuelMax 540 | fuelRate 1.0/s | durabilityMax 110 | deployRange 450 wu | cost 34000 cr | upkeep 30 cr/min
DRONE Mk4 | tier 4 | mineRate 5.5 u/s | bufferCap 480 u | fuelMax 720 | fuelRate 1.0/s | durabilityMax 180 | deployRange 500 wu | cost 90000 cr | upkeep 60 cr/min
TRADER Hauler-L | tier 1 | cargoVol 80 u | cycleTime 180 s | tradeEff 0.9 | hireCost 9000 cr | upkeep 18 cr/min | baseLossPerCycle 0.02
TRADER Freighter-M | tier 2 | cargoVol 200 u | cycleTime 240 s | tradeEff 0.92 | hireCost 28000 cr | upkeep 40 cr/min | baseLossPerCycle 0.025
TRADER Bulk-H | tier 3 | cargoVol 480 u | cycleTime 320 s | tradeEff 0.94 | hireCost 75000 cr | upkeep 85 cr/min | baseLossPerCycle 0.03
OUTPOST Ore Refinery | recipe: 2 ore -> 1 alloy | outRate 0.5 alloy/s @L1 | storageCap 300 @L1 | buildCost 60000 cr | defense 20 @L1 | upkeep 50 cr/min
OUTPOST Fuel Synth | recipe: 1 gas -> 1 fuel | outRate 0.7 fuel/s @L1 | storageCap 400 @L1 | buildCost 45000 cr | defense 15 @L1 | upkeep 40 cr/min
OUTPOST Hab/Trade Hub | recipe: passive credit gen (no input) | outRate 12 cr/s @L1 capped by storageCap-as-credit-buffer 1500 | buildCost 110000 cr | defense 30 @L1 | upkeep 90 cr/min
OUTPOST UPGRADE Lx | outRate x1.6/level | storageCap x1.7/level | defense +15/level | upgradeCost 0.8*buildCost*level | upkeep x1.5/level
FLEET ORDER mine | ship joins/forms a mining op at a field, acts as a high-tier drone (uses ship cargo as buffer, no fuel-loss but takes hull damage if attacked)
FLEET ORDER trade | ship runs an assigned 2-station route like a hired trader, cargoVol = ship cargo capacity, lower lossPerCycle (owned ships are tougher)
FLEET ORDER escort | ship follows player within 200 wu, adds its weapons to player-side combat (composes with combat subsystem)
FLEET ORDER guard | ship parks at an outpost or drone-group, multiplies that asset defense by 1.8 and halves its loss probability
FLEET CAP by tier | T1:2 | T2:3 | T3:4 | T4:6 | T5:8
ACTIVE REFERENCE A(T) cr/min | T1:250 | T2:600 | T3:1400 | T4:3200 | T5:7000 (designer constant, the anchor all passive yields are sized against)
formulas: Continuous accrual per tick (drones/outposts): bufferVol += clamp(rate * dt, 0, bufferCap - bufferVol). rate in u/s, dt in s.
Drone group gross value/min: grossDrones = sum_over_groups(min(mineRate*count, bufferFillRoom) * 60) * orePrice(oreType). Realized only on collect/recall.
Drone fuel: fuel -= fuelRate*dt each active tick; if fuel<=0 -> group LOST. Refuel cost on recall: refuelCost = (fuelMax-fuel) * 0.5 cr/unit.
Trader cycle profit: profit = cargoVol * max(0, sellPrice_B - buyPrice_A) * tradeEff - routeFuelCost. routeFuelCost = routeDist(A,B) * 0.4 cr/wu. Credited on cycleProgress reaching 1.0.
Trader cycleProgress per tick: cycleProgress += dt / cycleTime; on >=1.0 -> credit profit, roll loss, reset to 0.

Spread decay (self-limiting): after each completed cycle, emit 'economy:applyTradePressure' with {stationId:A, good, vol:+cargoVol} and {stationId:B, good, vol:-cargoVol}; economy subsystem moves prices, shrinking next cycle's spread.

Per-cycle loss probability: pLoss = clamp( baseLossPerCycle * dangerMult * hotnessMult / guardMult , 0, 0.35 ). dangerMult = 1 + sectorDanger(route)*2 (sectorDanger 0..1 read from sector data). hotnessMult = 1 + hotness (hotness rises +0.05 per consecutive cycle on same route, decays 0.1/min when idle). guardMult = 1 + 0.5*guardShipsOnRoute.
Trader loss resolution: on loss, remove trader, emit 'automation:assetLost' {kind:'trader',id,value}, refund 0; spawn pirate encounter flag in route sector (composes with combat/spawn).
Outpost continuous output: storage.vol += clamp(outRate(level)*dt, 0, storageCap(level)-storage.vol). outRate(level)=outRate_L1 * 1.6^(level-1). storageCap(level)=cap_L1 * 1.7^(level-1).
Outpost auto-sell income/min: if autoSell, sellable = min(storage.vol, outRate*60); income = sellable * localPrice(good) * 0.8; storage.vol -= sellable.

Outpost raid check (rolled every raidInterval=600s while danger>0): pRaid = clamp( sectorDanger * 0.4 / defenseMult , 0, 0.5 ). defenseMult = (defense(level)/20) * (guardShip?1.8:1). On success: storage.vol *= 0.3, set state 'raided' for raidCooldown=300s, emit 'automation:outpostRaided'.
Upkeep drain per tick: state.credits -= (sum(upkeepPerMin)/60)*dt. Track upkeepDebt if credits insufficient; at upkeepDebt accumulating > distressGraceSec worth -> repossess one random asset.

GLOBAL CAP enforcement (applied when crediting any realized passive income this minute): let net = grossRealized - upkeepThisMin - expectedLossThisMin; capLimit = passiveCapFrac * A(T_player); if net > capLimit then credited = capLimit + (net-capLimit)*overflowEff else credited = net.
Expected loss per min (for cap accounting): expLoss = sum_traders(pLoss * traderValue / (cycleTime/60)) + sum_outposts(pRaid * storageValue / (raidInterval/60)).

Offline catch-up on load: elapsed=clamp(now-lastSave,0,offlineCapSec); for each source compute capped accrual as above using elapsed instead of dt, multiply realized credits by offlineEff=0.6; traders complete floor(elapsed/cycleTime) cycles, apply aggregated survival = (1-pLoss)^cycles to decide if trader survived (single roll). Deduct upkeep*elapsed/60.

Net passive sizing target (verification): with passiveCapFrac=0.45, a fully-built tier-T automation stack credits <= 0.45*A(T) cr/min after upkeep+loss, i.e. always < active play; raw gross may exceed it but overflowEff=0.25 crushes returns past the cap.
interactions: READS state.credits (owned by economy/core, assumed number) — debits upkeep, credits passive income via accumulators to avoid sub-cr loss.
READS state.player.sectorId and state.player.pos:{x,z} (owned by core/movement) — for deploy-range checks and collect proximity.
READS state.player.progressionTier T (owned by progression subsystem, assumed int 1..5) — drives A(T), fleetCap, drone/trader/outpost tier availability.
READS commodity prices via query fn ctx.economy.getPrice(stationId, good) or event 'economy:priceQuery' (owned by economy subsystem, assumed shape) — trader profit & outpost auto-sell.
READS sector danger via ctx.world.getSectorDanger(sectorId)->0..1 (owned by world/faction subsystem, assumed) — loss & raid probabilities.

READS asteroid field data ctx.mining.getField(fieldId)->{asteroids[],orePrices} and reuses ctx.mining.mineAsteroid(astId,vol) (owned by mining subsystem) — drones reuse the same mining resolution as the player.
READS ship cargo capacity ctx.cargo.freeSpace(shipId) (owned by cargo subsystem) — collect transfer and fleet 'mine' buffer.
EMITS 'economy:applyTradePressure' {stationId,good,vol} — moves prices so trader routes self-limit.
EMITS 'automation:assetLost' {kind,id,value,sectorId} — notifications subsystem shows alert; combat/spawn may spawn the pirate that killed it.
EMITS 'automation:outpostRaided' {outpostId,sectorId,lossVol} — notifications + optional defend mission hook (missions subsystem).
EMITS 'automation:assetDistressed' {kind,id} and 'automation:assetRepossessed' {kind,id} — UI warnings when upkeep unpaid.
HANDLES 'combat:hitAsset' {assetKind,assetId,damage} — combat subsystem reports hits on drones/fleet/outposts; this system applies durability/hp loss and may trigger LOST.
HANDLES 'ui:fleetOrder' {shipId,order,targetRef} from Fleet UI — sets order with redeployTimer.
WRITES into save blob: entire automation.* tree serialized to JSON by save/load; lastSaveTime stamped for offline catch-up.
Update order: runs AFTER economy (needs current prices) and mining (needs field state), BEFORE notifications/UI refresh (so alerts reflect this tick).
uiNeeds: AUTOMATION / FLEET panel (DOM overlay, opened from HUD or station menu): tabbed Drones | Traders | Outposts | Fleet.

Drones tab: list of drone groups with sector, count, ore type, buffer fill bar (vol/bufferCap), fuel bar, durability bar, state badge; actions Deploy (when in field), Recall, Refuel, toggle Auto-return; live cr/min estimate per group.

Traders tab: list of traders with route (A->B), tier, cycle progress ring, last-cycle profit, hotness meter, danger badge, upkeep; actions Re-route, Recall/Dismiss, assign Guard; net cr/min after upkeep.
Outposts tab: list with type/recipe, level, storage fill bar, defense value, raid-risk badge, autoSell toggle, upkeep; actions Upgrade (shows cost), Restock, assign Guard ship; cr/min.
Fleet tab: roster of owned ships (max fleetCap shown as x/cap), each with current order dropdown (mine/trade/escort/guard/idle), target selector, redeploy timer, hp bar; ship acquisition entry point.
Global passive summary header: total gross cr/min, total upkeep cr/min, expected loss cr/min, NET cr/min, and a CAP bar showing net vs passiveCapFrac*A(T) (turns amber when overflow-throttled).
HUD ticker: small scrolling line for 'automation:incomeCredited' and red flash for 'automation:assetLost'/'outpostRaided'/'assetDistressed'.
Map overlay markers: icons for owned drone groups, trader routes (line A-B with danger color), and outposts, so the management/attention cost is spatially visible.
Offline-return modal on load: 'While away (capped Xh): +N cr from drones, +M cr from traders, K cycles run, J assets lost' summary.
risks: Cap correctness: the global passive cap must be enforced at credit time, not estimated; if overflowEff or expectedLoss accounting is wrong, stacked automation can exceed active income and break the core fantasy. Unit-test net<=0.45*A(T) across full builds per tier.

Borrowed-state shape mismatch: economy price query, sector danger, mining field access, cargo capacity, and progression tier are all owned elsewhere with assumed shapes; orchestrator must reconcile exact fn/event names or these silently no-op.

Offline catch-up exploitation: clamping (OFFLINE_CAP=4h) and offlineEff=0.6 must be airtight; an off-by-one in cycle count or an uncapped buffer lets players time-skip for huge gains. Also guard against negative elapsed from clock changes.
Per-tick fractional credits: crediting/draining sub-1-cr amounts at 60Hz must use accumulators (creditBuffer/upkeepDebt) or rounding loses money or duplicates it.

Trade-pressure feedback loop: if economy doesn't actually move prices on 'applyTradePressure', trader routes won't self-limit and a single route becomes infinite money — needs the economy subsystem to honor the event.
Loss-event UX: assets vanishing while the player is elsewhere must always notify clearly (and ideally be preventable via guard), or losses feel unfair/random rather than a managed risk.
Distress repossession: the soft-failure repossession on unpaid upkeep must pick low-value or clearly-flagged assets and warn first, or it feels like a punitive wipe.
Determinism: loss/raid rolls should draw from a seeded RNG in GameState so saves/replays stay deterministic and offline catch-up is reproducible.
